title: Sony will stop manufacturing physical discs for PlayStation in 2028
summary: The Japanese company ends the physical format in its consoles, eliminating the production of disc games as of January 2028.

title: US lifts export restrictions on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models
summary: After two weeks of prohibition, the US government again allows global use of these advanced artificial intelligence models.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5, its most affordable AI model for agents
summary: The new model offers improved reasoning and coding at a lower cost, competing directly with other market alternatives.

title: Google presents Nano Banana 2 Lite, a fast and cheap image generator, along with Gemini Omni Flash for video
summary: Google's new generative AI models are available via API and offer reduced prices for developers and users.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Science, an AI workspace for researchers
summary: The platform is designed to facilitate scientific work with integrated language models in a dedicated environment.

title: South Korea will invest more than 500 billion dollars in chips and memory for AI alongside Samsung and SK Hynix
summary: The plan seeks to dominate the semiconductor and robotics market through massive investment in infrastructure and development.

title: PS5 Linux now works on PlayStation 5 Slim with firmware 7.61
summary: Linux compatibility expands to more console models, including the Slim version.

title: Xbox restructuring leads to layoffs and the creation of a union of 3,500 employees
summary: Affected studios suffer cuts, while workers organize against mass layoffs in Microsoft's gaming division.

title: NVIDIA RTX 5050 9GB could be canceled or delayed indefinitely
summary: Graphics card manufacturers have no information about the launch of this entry-level model.

title: The Steam Machine improves its performance by 2.5% with dual-channel RAM
summary: Tests show that dual-channel memory usage offers a slight gain in graphics tests, although Valve does not officially recommend it.

title: NVIDIA optimizes its Blackwell GPUs for DeepSeek, reducing token cost by up to 80%
summary: The collaboration between NVIDIA and DeepSeek allows Chinese AI to run more efficiently on NVIDIA hardware.

title: Claude Code quietly marks requests from Chinese users and hides code in its responses
summary: It is discovered that Anthropic's code assistant includes steganographic marks that allow identifying the origin of the requests.

title: Meta plans to sell excess AI compute capacity in the style of SpaceX
summary: The company seeks to monetize its artificial intelligence infrastructure by offering server power to third parties.

title: OpenClaw launches native apps for iOS and Android
summary: The remote access service will be officially available on mobile devices.

title: Samsung shows the redesign of the Galaxy Z Fold8 with a wider screen
summary: Official leaks and teasers point to a significant change in the design of the foldable.

title: GTA VI will not have a physical version, targets 30 FPS and would sell more on PS5 according to reports
summary: The lack of a disc, limited performance, and expected sales figures mark the latest news about the anticipated game.

title: Collector shows 40 years of ATI-AMD GPUs while another disassembles more than 40 old graphics cards
summary: Two independent initiatives celebrate the history of graphics hardware with extensive collections and disassemblies.

title: AMD will raise prices of its GPU and memory bundles by 10% in July due to GDDR6 shortage
summary: EXPO ULL memory kits become up to 79% more expensive than regular EXPO kits, reflecting the tension in the memory market.

title: Microsoft tests a smaller taskbar in Windows 11 and reduces its Surface Go and Surface Laptop Go line
summary: The company adjusts its operating system and device catalog to focus on more powerful options.

title: Intel launches driver support for Arc G3 Extreme while acknowledging Path Tracing issues on its Arc B570 and B580 GPUs
summary: The Intel Arc graphics family receives driver updates but presents crashes when enabling Path Tracing without low VRAM.
